1.1.1.title: Non-text Content
1.1.1.short: All non-text content has a text alternative that serves the equivalent purpose
1.1.1.indian_context: Critical for screen reader users. India has 40M+ visually impaired people who rely on alt text.
1.1.1.common_fix: Add descriptive alt attribute to img elements; use aria-label for icon buttons

1.3.1.title: Info and Relationships
1.3.1.short: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined
1.3.1.indian_context: Essential for government forms and banking applications. Proper heading structure aids navigation.
1.3.1.common_fix: Use semantic HTML (h1-h6, lists, tables); associate form labels with inputs

1.4.1.title: Use of Color
1.4.1.short: Color is not used as the only visual means of conveying information
1.4.1.indian_context: 8% of Indian males have color vision deficiency. Never use red/green alone for error/success states.
1.4.1.common_fix: Add text labels, icons, or patterns alongside color indicators

1.4.3.title: Contrast (Minimum)
1.4.3.short: Text has a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 (3:1 for large text)
1.4.3.indian_context: Important for users in varying light conditions common in India. Low-end displays need higher contrast.
1.4.3.common_fix: Increase text color darkness or background lightness; use contrast checker tools

1.4.4.title: Resize Text
1.4.4.short: Text can be resized up to 200% without loss of content or functionality
1.4.4.indian_context: Aging population and limited access to eye care make text resizing essential for Indian users.
1.4.4.common_fix: Use relative units (rem, em) instead of px; test with browser zoom at 200%

1.4.10.title: Reflow
1.4.10.short: Content reflows without horizontal scrolling at 320px width
1.4.10.indian_context: High mobile usage in India (700M+ users). Most access web on small screens.
1.4.10.common_fix: Use responsive design; avoid fixed widths; use CSS flexbox/grid

1.4.11.title: Non-text Contrast
1.4.11.short: UI components and graphics have at least 3:1 contrast ratio
1.4.11.indian_context: Form inputs and buttons must be visible on low-quality mobile displays common in India.
1.4.11.common_fix: Ensure borders, icons, and focus indicators meet 3:1 contrast against adjacent colors

1.4.12.title: Text Spacing
1.4.12.short: No loss of content when text spacing is adjusted
1.4.12.indian_context: Users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities may need increased spacing. Common in Indian workforce.
1.4.12.common_fix: Avoid fixed heights on text containers; use flexible CSS

2.1.1.title: Keyboard
2.1.1.short: All functionality is operable through a keyboard interface
2.1.1.indian_context: Many Indian users rely on keyboard due to motor disabilities or assistive technology.
2.1.1.common_fix: Ensure all interactive elements are focusable; use native HTML buttons and links

2.1.2.title: No Keyboard Trap
2.1.2.short: Keyboard focus can be moved away from any component using standard keys
2.1.2.indian_context: Keyboard traps prevent users from accessing other page content. Critical for accessibility.
2.1.2.common_fix: Ensure Tab moves through all elements; Escape closes modals; avoid focus loops

2.4.1.title: Bypass Blocks
2.4.1.short: A mechanism is available to bypass blocks of content repeated on multiple pages
2.4.1.indian_context: Skip links essential for government portals with complex navigation. Required by GIGW 3.0.
2.4.1.common_fix: Add 'Skip to main content' link as first focusable element on every page

2.4.3.title: Focus Order
2.4.3.short: Focusable components receive focus in an order that preserves meaning
2.4.3.indian_context: Logical tab order is essential for form completion on Indian banking/government sites.
2.4.3.common_fix: Use natural DOM order; avoid positive tabindex values; test with Tab key

2.4.4.title: Link Purpose (In Context)
2.4.4.short: The purpose of each link can be determined from the link text alone or together with context
2.4.4.indian_context: Avoid 'Click here' and 'Read more' - screen reader users need descriptive links.
2.4.4.common_fix: Use descriptive link text like 'Download Annual Report 2024' instead of 'Click here'

2.4.7.title: Focus Visible
2.4.7.short: Keyboard focus indicator is visible on all interactive elements
2.4.7.indian_context: Required for keyboard navigation. Never remove outline without replacement.
2.4.7.common_fix: Use visible outline on :focus-visible; minimum 2px solid with good contrast

2.4.11.title: Focus Not Obscured (Minimum)
2.4.11.short: When a component receives focus, it is not entirely hidden by author-created content
2.4.11.indian_context: New in WCAG 2.2. Sticky headers must not cover focused elements.
2.4.11.common_fix: Use scroll-margin-top on focusable elements; ensure sticky elements don't obscure focus

2.5.3.title: Label in Name
2.5.3.short: UI components with visible text have accessible names that include the visible text
2.5.3.indian_context: Voice control users say what they see. Button labeled 'Submit' must have accessible name 'Submit'.
2.5.3.common_fix: Ensure aria-label starts with or matches visible text label

2.5.8.title: Target Size (Minimum)
2.5.8.short: Touch targets are at least 24x24 CSS pixels with adequate spacing
2.5.8.indian_context: New in WCAG 2.2. Essential for mobile users with motor impairments. 44x44px recommended.
2.5.8.common_fix: Increase button/link padding; use min-height: 44px for touch targets

3.1.1.title: Language of Page
3.1.1.short: The default human language of each page can be programmatically determined
3.1.1.indian_context: Critical for multilingual India. Screen readers need lang attribute to pronounce correctly.
3.1.1.common_fix: Add lang='en' or lang='hi' to html element; use BCP 47 language codes

3.3.1.title: Error Identification
3.3.1.short: If an input error is automatically detected, the item in error is identified and described in text
3.3.1.indian_context: Clear error messages essential for Indian users filling government/banking forms.
3.3.1.common_fix: Display error text near the field; use aria-describedby to associate; announce with aria-live

4.1.2.title: Name, Role, Value
4.1.2.short: All UI components have accessible name, role, and state that can be programmatically set
4.1.2.indian_context: Assistive technology must understand all interactive elements. Critical for NVDA/JAWS users.
4.1.2.common_fix: Use native HTML elements; add ARIA attributes only when necessary; test with screen reader
